purpose: Soundness-coverage ledger. Every cryptographic verify/proof function declares its SECURITY INVARIANT (what 'verify==OK' must imply) and a NEGATIVE-SOUNDNESS probe: a test that forges an input VIOLATING the invariant and asserts the verifier REJECTS it. A function with a claim but no wired probe is a potential hole of the GHSA-c7q2 class (a verifying-but-unsound input that every positive roundtrip test misses). Enforced by ci/check_soundness_coverage.py.
methodology: Invert the invariant: do not test that correct inputs are accepted (roundtrip) — test that INCORRECT inputs are blocked. If the system cannot block the wrong case, that is the hole.

invariants.claim: verify==OK => log_G(R_hat) == log_T(R): the pre-signature's two nonce points share the same secret k, so r=R.x is bound to the adaptor base T and the pre-signature is adaptable.
invariants.forge: Construct R_hat=k1*G, R=k2*T with k1!=k2 that still satisfies r==R.x AND the ECDSA relation s_hat*R_hat==z*G+r*P (choose s_hat=(z+r*x)*k1^-1). Only the Chaum-Pedersen DLEQ can reject it.

invariants.claim: verify==OK => log_G(P) == log_H(Q): the prover knows a single x with P=x*G and Q=x*H.
invariants.forge: Construct P=x1*G, Q=x2*H with x1!=x2 and any (e,s); a sound verifier recomputes e'=H(G,H,P,Q,R1,R2) and rejects (e'!=e).

invariants.claim: verify==OK => the prover knows the discrete log x of the public point P=x*base.
invariants.forge: A proof (rx, s) for a P whose discrete log the forger does not know must fail s*base - e*P reconstructing to rx with even Y.

invariants.claim: verify==OK => adapt(pre, t) yields a valid BIP-340 signature for T=t*G, and extract(pre, full) recovers t.
invariants.forge: Pre-signature whose R_hat/T relationship is inconsistent with the challenge e=H((R_hat+T).x||P.x||m) must fail s*G==R_hat+e*P.

invariants.claim: verify==OK => s_i*G == R_i + b*R2_i + e*a_i*g*gacc*P_i, binding the partial sig to (aggnonce, aggpk, msg) so the Wagner/ROS forgery cannot complete.
invariants.forge: Partial sig with a nonce/binding-coefficient not derived from H('MuSig/noncecoef', R1||R2||Q_x||msg) must be rejected.

invariants.claim: verify==OK => z_i*G == R_i + lambda_i*e*Y_i, binding z_i to the group commitment R, verification share Y_i, Lagrange coeff and message.
invariants.forge: Partial sig with a binding factor rho_i not derived from the full commitment list, or an absent-signer share, must be rejected.

invariants.claim: verify==OK => the Pedersen-committed value lies in [0, 2^n): a prover cannot prove a commitment to an out-of-range value.
invariants.forge: A proof for a commitment to value >= 2^n (or negative) must be rejected; also fuzz the Fiat-Shamir transcript (see kb BP-FS-IP-CHAIN).
